notes.note: "Imperator" was the salutation a victorious general's own soldiers conferred on him after a battle; it qualified its holder to seek a triumph. Decimus claims he campaigned to bind his troops' loyalty, not to win the acclamation for its own sake.
Mountain peoples on the borders of Cisalpine Gaul, the province Decimus Brutus governed. He fought them in late 44 BC chiefly to harden his army for the coming struggle with Antony.
